page: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with multi-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It includes 3686 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% on stress-ng multi-core tests. Cryptographic capabilities are also in the top 10% for PassMark CPU encryption, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets. Memory performance is highly competitive, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and cached small-block read bandwidth in the top 25%, though uncached large-block read and write speeds remain in the average tier. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and Geekbench image processing, demonstrate strong to top-tier execution speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96ads_v5 offers high resource density by combining a substantial 96-vCPU compute allocation with a large 3686 GB local storage volume, reducing the immediate need for external block storage attachments. This resource balance makes the instance highly effective for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks. It is particularly optimized for enterprise database workloads, redis caching layers, and parallelized data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 384.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server includes 3686 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside strong memory performance and database execution speeds. Uncached large-block memory bandwidth remains average. Qualitatively, the server provides high resource density by bundling massive compute capacity with substantial local storage, mitigating the need for external storage add-ons. It is optimized for enterprise database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and parallel data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v5 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose VM with 384 GB RAM and 3686 GB local storage, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 server combining ninety-six AMD vCPUs with substantial local storage for database operations and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores
Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM and 3686 GB of local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cryptographic encryption capabilities
Strong database operations and multi-threaded decompression benchmark results
Optimized for enterprise databases, software compilation, and parallel data processing
